reasoning: Both outputs are high-quality contracts with 31 postconditions across 4 layers, 7 invariants, 7+ NOT-in-scope items, traceability matrices with Status columns, and quality gate scores of 11/11. Output A is the marginal winner on three key dimensions: (1) Completeness — Output A covers all 4 API endpoints stated in the eval prompt, including the stats endpoint (GET /api/shopify-orders/stats) with dedicated postconditions (PC-A9, PC-S10) and a ShopifyOrdersStats React component, while Output B only contracts 3 GET endpoints and misses the stats endpoint entirely. (2) Anti-tautology rigor — Output A includes 10 explicitly annotated expect() skeletons with 'Anti-tautology:' comments explaining exactly which code deletion would cause each test to fail; Output B's skeletons are correct but lack this traceability annotation. (3) Error coverage — Output A has 12 ERR-N entries vs Output B's 11, including explicit coverage of resolveTenantId() failure (ERR-12), Melbourne timezone boundary effects, and a pageSize cap edge case. Output B has genuine strengths Output A lacks: it includes full database DDL with indices (immediately implementable), stores prices as integer cents avoiding float precision issues (a safer design choice), and isolates HMAC verification into a dedicated utility (shopifyHmac.js) with its own postconditions including PC-S3 specifically testing timingSafeEqual. However, missing the stats endpoint — one of the 4 stated API endpoints — is a completeness gap that tips the decision to Output A.

strengths: Covers all 4 API endpoints including the stats endpoint (GET /api/shopify-orders/stats) with dedicated PCs and a ShopifyOrdersStats React component
10 explicitly annotated anti-tautology skeletons with 'Anti-tautology:' comments explaining which deletion causes failure
12 error cases including resolveTenantId() failure, Melbourne timezone boundary, pageSize cap, and IDOR via cross-tenant access
Melbourne timezone explicitly contracted in PC-S10 and ERR-10 (CURRENT_DATE AT TIME ZONE 'Australia/Melbourne')
Error strategy matrix covers 10 operations with retry policy and transaction boundary diagram
Blast radius includes 5 same-file siblings, 3 cross-file siblings, 2 validation function siblings, and 9 edge cases — all with specific file:line
Single write path rule explicitly stated: orderPersistenceService.js is the ONLY file that writes to either table
31 PCs with traceability matrix including Status column and zero orphans
weaknesses: Does not include concrete database DDL schema — implementer must infer schema from postconditions and invariants
Prices stored as floats rather than integer cents — potential floating-point precision issue not addressed
HMAC utility is embedded in the handler rather than extracted to a dedicated testable module

strengths: Includes complete DDL for both tables with indices — immediately implementable without inference
Prices stored as integer cents (total_price_cents) eliminating floating-point precision risk; PC-S5 tests the conversion explicitly
HMAC verification extracted to dedicated shopifyHmac.js utility with its own PCs (PC-S1, PC-S2, PC-S3) including explicit timingSafeEqual test
Idempotency design explicitly documented with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING strategy and RETURNING id handling for the skip case
Blast radius cross-file siblings include productService.js and supplierService.js with specific line references confirming existing tenant-scoping pattern
File line estimates provided for all new files — aids sprint planning
31 PCs with traceability matrix including Status column and zero orphans
7 NOT-in-scope items covering all major risk areas
weaknesses: Missing the stats endpoint (GET /api/shopify-orders/stats) — the eval prompt specifies 4 API endpoints and the stats endpoint is one of them; Output B only contracts 3
No ShopifyOrdersStats component — the eval prompt specifies 3 React components; Output B's 3 components (OrdersPage, OrdersTable, OrderDetailPanel) differ from the plan's stated scope
Anti-tautology annotations are implicit (skeletons assert specific values) but not explicitly labeled — makes tautology audit harder
Melbourne timezone not mentioned anywhere — omitted from normalization, persistence, and stats contracts
